questions.question: Aim
questions.answer:
• To create an abstract class BankAccount with a protected variable balance and an abstract method calculateInterest(). [cite: 906]
  • Implement child classes SavingAccount (5% interest) and CurrentAccount (2% interest) and display calculated interest for a balance of Rs. 10,000. [cite: 907]

questions.question: Code
questions.answer:
balance = $balance;
  }
  abstract public function calculateInterest();
}

class SavingAccount extends BankAccount {
  public function calculateInterest() {
    $interest = $this->balance * 0.05;
    echo "Saving Account Interest: " . $interest . "
";
  }
}

class CurrentAccount extends BankAccount {
  public function calculateInterest() {
    $interest = $this->balance * 0.02;
    echo "Current Account Interest: " . $interest . "
";
  }
}

$saving = new SavingAccount(10000);
$current = new CurrentAccount(10000);
$saving->calculateInterest();
$current->calculateInterest();
?>

questions.question: Aim
questions.answer: • To demonstrate how a React.js frontend can fetch data from a Node.js/Express backend server using HTTP requests (Fetch API), simulating a real-world full-stack application. [cite: 1244]

questions.question: Backend Code (server.js)
questions.answer:
const express = require("express");
const cors = require("cors");
const app = express();
app.use(cors());
const PORT = 5000;

const students = [
  { id: 1, name: "Rahul", course: "BCA" },
  { id: 2, name: "Priya", course: "B.Tech" }
];

app.get("/students", (req, res) => {
  res.json(students);
});
app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Server running on http://localhost:${PORT}`);
});
